mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: No printer enrollment methods found in this batch. Printer enrollment functionality likely exists in enrollment helper or printer management modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription state verification methods found in this batch. Subscription management methods likely exist in subscription helper or API helper modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription status verification methods found in this batch. Subscription state checking methods likely exist in subscription helper or API helper modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Navigates to the dashboard portal and handles login if needed. This can be used to access the dashboard where the Pause Instant Ink link may be located.
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This precondition requires navigating to the dashboard to access pause plan functionality. The access method provides dashboard navigation which is a prerequisite for finding and clicking the Pause Instant Ink link.

precondition_mapping.notes: Found dashboard access method which helps navigate to where pause plan controls are located, but the actual pause plan button/link locators and click methods are not in this batch. Pause plan page objects and interaction methods likely exist in other batches.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Generates and returns the current timestamp as a formatted string in the pattern YYYY_MM_DD_HH_MM_SS. Used for naming output files and run identifiers.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Useful for generating unique identifiers for test run outputs, screenshots, and reports throughout the test execution.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Sanitizes a filename string by replacing all invalid filesystem characters with underscores. Returns the sanitized filename string safe for use in file operations.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Useful for sanitizing filenames when saving screenshots or other test artifacts during the test execution.

matched_methods.method_signature: def take_element_screenshot(page: Page, selector: str, output_path: str) -> bool
matched_methods.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of a specific element on the page identified by a selector and saves it to the specified path. Returns True on success, False on failure.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 20 requires capturing screenshots of the Billing Cycle Period card for visual verification. This method can capture element-specific screenshots for comparison.

matched_methods.method_signature: def save_full_page_screenshot(page: Page, flow_name: str, context_name: str, output_dir: str = None) -> str
matched_methods.what_it_does: Captures a full-page screenshot and saves it with a timestamped filename. Returns the filename. Used for validation checkpoints to capture the complete page state.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 20 involves visual verification of the Billing Cycle Period card layout. This method captures full-page screenshots for visual comparison against baseline images.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Disables scrolling on a web page by injecting CSS that sets overflow to hidden. Useful when capturing screenshots to prevent the page from scrolling during automation.
matched_methods.usage_example: freeze_scroll(page)  # Disables scrolling on the page
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 20 requires visual verification with screenshot capture. Freezing scroll ensures stable screenshots without page movement during capture.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Re-enables scrolling on a web page by injecting CSS that sets overflow to auto. Restores normal scrolling behavior after it was disabled.
matched_methods.usage_example: unfreeze_scroll(page)  # Re-enables scrolling on the page
matched_methods.relevance_reason: After capturing screenshots in step 20, this method restores normal scrolling behavior for continued test execution.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Extracts the visible inner text from a Playwright element and returns it as a trimmed string. Respects CSS visibility and display properties.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 8, 11, 14, 18, and 19 require reading and verifying text values from complimentary pages, additional pages, and total pages printed displays. This method extracts text for comparison.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Checks if a Playwright element is hidden or disabled by evaluating JavaScript. Returns True if element is hidden via CSS or disabled.
matched_methods.usage_example: is_hidden = is_element_hidden_or_disabled(element_handle)  # Returns True if hidden or disabled
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 2 verifies plan pause information is NOT displayed, and step 5 verifies it IS displayed. This method can check element visibility state.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Retrieves the position and dimensions of a Playwright element as a dictionary with x, y, width, and height keys.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 21 involves responsive layout verification. This method can retrieve element positions to verify proper layout across different viewport sizes.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Extracts computed CSS styles from a Playwright element including font_family, font_size, font_weight, color, background, and text_align properties.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Steps 11, 14, and 18 require verifying progress bar colors (black for complimentary, yellow for additional). This method extracts CSS styles including background color for verification.

matched_methods.method_signature: def validate_context(flow_name, context_name, page, excel_path, output_folder, validation_workbook_path=None, screenshot_only=False, sub_context=None, lang_code=None, animations=False, target_dir=None)
matched_methods.what_it_does: Main validation function that compares live page state against baseline scraped data with comprehensive element, text, screenshot, and animation validation. Supports screenshot-only mode for quick checkpoints.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This is the core validation engine that can be used to validate the entire Print and Payment History page state against baseline data. It handles element validation, text comparison, screenshot comparison, and can be used for comprehensive validation of all visual and functional aspects of the Billing Cycle Period card.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Performs pixel-by-pixel image comparison with exclusion region filtering and bounding box generation for failures. Returns (is_pass, diff, boxes, failed_pixels) tuple.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 20 requires visual verification by comparing screenshots against baseline. This method performs detailed pixel-by-pixel comparison with support for exclusion regions to ignore dynamic content.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Comprehensive static helper class providing utility methods for all dashboard-related test operations including dashboard access, navigation, first-time setup with modal handling, and various dashboard UI interactions.
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: Provides dashboard access methods which are needed to navigate to the Print and Payment History page. The access method can be used as part of precondition setup and for navigation in steps 1 and 4.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Static helper class providing utility methods for testing subscription cancellation flows. While primarily focused on cancellation, it may contain methods for pause plan functionality which is related to this test case.
matched_helpers.usage_example: # Methods would be called as CancellationPlanHelper.method_name(page, args)
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This helper contains cancellation and pause plan related methods. While specific pause plan methods were not detailed in the batch analysis, this helper is relevant to precondition 3 which requires pausing the plan.

test_case_mappings.overall_notes: Batch 7 provides excellent coverage for visual verification, screenshot capture, and validation infrastructure. Found comprehensive utilities for timestamp generation, filename sanitization, screenshot capture (both element and full-page), scroll control, text extraction, style extraction, element position checking, and image comparison with exclusion handling. The core validator module provides the main validation engine. Dashboard helper provides navigation to dashboard which is a prerequisite for accessing Print and Payment History. However, this batch does NOT contain: (1) Print and Payment History page object with specific locators for billing cycle card, progress bars, tooltips, info icons, and page usage displays, (2) Time manipulation and billing charge trigger APIs, (3) Print job simulation APIs, (4) Page refresh methods, (5) Pause plan setup methods, (6) Printer enrollment methods, (7) Subscription state management, (8) Viewport resizing for responsive testing, (9) Device-specific interaction methods (hover vs click). These components are likely in other batches containing page objects, API helpers, printer helpers, and responsive testing utilities. Step 20 (visual verification) has the best coverage with all necessary screenshot and comparison utilities found.
